compiled_state.title: Fix cache contract: use flat five_hour_pct/seven_day_pct fields
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Fix the cache JSON format in check_usage.py. Currently writes nested objects like {"five_hour": {"utilization": 0.85}}. The done definition requires flat fields: {"five_hour_pct": 0.85, "seven_day_pct": 0.72, "timestamp": 1234567890, "resets_at": "..."}.

Fix: In write_cache(), extract utilization floats and store as five_hour_pct/seven_day_pct. Update read_cache() and all consumers (main() lines that read cached data) to use the flat field names. Update the module docstring to document the correct schema.

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/scripts/check_usage.py (modify — write_cache, read_cache consumers, docstring)
- tests/scripts/test_check_usage.py (modify — update cache test assertions)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cache JSON uses flat five_hour_pct/seven_day_pct fields
  Verify: python3 -c "import json,tempfile,sys; sys.path.insert(0,'plugins/dso/scripts'); from check_usage import write_cache, read_cache; f=tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.json'); write_cache(f,{'five_hour':{'utilization':0.5},'seven_day':{'utilization':0.3}}); d=read_cache(f); assert 'five_hour_pct' in d, f'missing five_hour_pct: {d.keys()}'"
- [ ] All 16 unit tests pass
  Verify: python3 -m pytest tests/scripts/test_check_usage.py --tb=short -q
- [ ] Module docstring documents correct cache schema
  Verify: grep -q "five_hour_pct" plugins/dso/scripts/check_usage.py

comments.body: CHECKPOINT: Cache JSON format fix complete. write_cache() now flattens nested API data to {five_hour_pct, seven_day_pct, timestamp, resets_at}. main() cache-read paths updated to flat fields. Tests updated and passing (16 pytest + 21 bash assertions). Ruff clean.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓ — Cache contract flattened. Commit 0de6d5d4.
